vectors.name: STR$ Function
vectors.description: Returns a string representation of the value of a numeric expression.

quirks_and_tests.description: CRITICAL QUIRK: Positive numbers and zero are strictly prefixed with a leading space (acting as an implicit '+' sign). Negative numbers are prefixed by '-' without a leading space.

vectors.name: HEX$ Function
vectors.description: Returns a string that represents the hexadecimal value of the decimal argument.

quirks_and_tests.description: QBasic rounds floats to the nearest integer before conversion (Banker's rounding). Standard numbers are treated as 16-bit integers (FFFF for -1), while Long integers (&) or values exceeding -32768 fallback to 32-bit two's complement. Output is always uppercase.

vectors.name: VAL Function
vectors.description: Returns the numeric value of a string of characters.

quirks_and_tests.description: Accurately parses MS-DOS legacy radix prefixes: Hexadecimal (&H) and Octal (&O). Invalid input gracefully falls back to 0 without throwing an exception.

vectors.name: LEFT$ Function
vectors.description: Returns a string consisting of the leftmost n% characters of a string.

quirks_and_tests.description: If N exceeds the string length, the entire string is safely returned without throwing an out-of-bounds error.

vectors.name: RIGHT$ Function
vectors.description: Returns a string consisting of the rightmost n% characters of a string.

quirks_and_tests.description: Similar to LEFT$, an oversized N returns the whole string safely.

vectors.name: MID$ Function
vectors.description: Returns a substring of a specified length starting at a 1-based index.

quirks_and_tests.description: If the length parameter is omitted, it returns the remainder of the string. Out-of-bounds start indices yield an empty string without throwing an error.

vectors.name: SPACE$ Function
vectors.description: Returns a string of n% spaces.

quirks_and_tests.description: Negative or zero lengths safely return an empty string.

vectors.name: STRING$ Function
vectors.description: Returns a string of characters, n% long, consisting of a single character.

quirks_and_tests.description: The character can be specified either as a numeric ASCII/CP437 code, or as a string (in which case only the first character is used).

vectors.name: LEN Function
vectors.description: Returns the number of characters in a string.

vectors.name: UCASE$ Function
vectors.description: Returns a string expression with all letters converted to uppercase.

quirks_and_tests.description: Transforms lowercase ASCII characters to uppercase without affecting symbols or numbers.

vectors.name: LCASE$ Function
vectors.description: Returns a string expression with all letters converted to lowercase.

quirks_and_tests.description: Transforms uppercase ASCII characters to lowercase without affecting symbols or numbers.

vectors.name: LTRIM$ Function
vectors.description: Returns a copy of a string with leading (leftmost) spaces removed.

quirks_and_tests.description: Crucial for cleaning up STR$ outputs which naturally contain leading spaces for positive numbers.

vectors.name: RTRIM$ Function
vectors.description: Returns a copy of a string with trailing (rightmost) spaces removed.

quirks_and_tests.description: Often used to trim fixed-length strings (STRING * N) that are padded with spaces on the right.

vectors.name: CHR$ Function
vectors.description: Returns a one-character string whose ASCII/CP437 code is the argument.

quirks_and_tests.description: Sysclone maps these directly to the MS-DOS Code Page 437 (CP437) for graphical block character fidelity.

vectors.name: ASC Function
vectors.description: Returns the ASCII/CP437 character code corresponding to the first letter in a string.

quirks_and_tests.description: If the string is empty, true QBasic throws an 'Illegal function call' error.

vectors.name: INSTR Function
vectors.description: Returns the character position of the first occurrence of a string in another.

quirks_and_tests.description: Uses 1-based indexing. If an optional start index is provided, the search begins there. Returns 0 if the substring is not found or if the start index is out of bounds.
